news.title: 82,000 Argentina fans sign petition calling for World Cup Final replay over referee controversy
news.lede: Thousands of furious Argentina supporters are calling for the FIFA World Cup Final to be replayed following what they describe as a "corrupt" officiating display. Argentina fell 1-0 to Spain in extra time of the tournament showpiece in New Jersey on Sunday. Ferran Torres' strike proved decisive for the commanding Spanish side, who relentlessly pressured the Argentina goal throughout the game. Defending champions Argentina failed to register a single shot on target in regulation and were reduced to 10 men after Enzo Fernández received a red card in the 93rd minute.

news.title: Norway considers filing Fifa ethics complaint over Trump role in World Cup red card saga
news.lede: Norwegian federation president: Fifa on a ‘slippery slope’. Trump called Infantino for review of Balogun’s red. Donald Trump’s role in clearing Folarin Balogun from a mandatory one-game ban at the World Cup is set to provoke a formal complaint to Fifa ethics investigators. The Norway soccer federation said Thursday its board of directors will consider filing the complaint at its next meeting, likely on 6 August.

While England’s World Cup ended in disappointment, third place marked their best finish since 1966. The Three Lions secured the bronze medal with a 6-4 win over France in Miami, Florida, having been beaten 2-1 by Argentina in Atlanta, Georgia.

news.title: The extreme decision by Leandro Paredes after his controversial participation in the World Cup final
news.lede: South American football never lets up, and this time the news is echoing loudly through the offices of the Xeneize club. After a grueling international fixture that brought the entire planet to a standstill, alarms were raised over the physical condition of one of its biggest stars. According to The Clinic, Leandro Paredes took an inflexible stance by directly asking head coach Rodolfo Arruabarrena to be in the starting lineup for the Copa Sudamericana clash against O’Higgins de Rancagua. The news caused a stir given that the midfielder is coming off playing in the 2026 World Cup final against Spain and is carrying a painful issue with his ribs.

news.title: Norwegian Football Federation targets FIFA over Trump's World Cup intervention, and it matters beyond the pitch
news.lede: The ethics complaint over Balogun's lifted ban raises uncomfortable questions about political interference in global institutions, a pattern crypto markets know all too well. The Norwegian Football Federation is preparing to file a formal ethics complaint with FIFA over what it sees as a breathtaking breach of institutional integrity: US President Donald Trump allegedly called FIFA president Gianni Infantino to get a player’s suspension reversed during the 2026 World Cup. NFF president Lise Klaveness plans to seek board approval at a meeting on August 6 to move forward with the complaint. The target isn’t just Trump’s phone call.

news.title: Folarin Balogun: Norway consider formal complaint to Fifa
news.lede: Folarin Balogun was the United States' top scorer at the World Cup with three goals. The Norwegian Football Federation is considering filing a formal complaint to Fifa's ethics committee about the decision to suspend Folarin Balogun's automatic one-match ban during the World Cup. United States forward Balogun was expected to miss his side's last-16 tie against Belgium after being shown a straight red card during their 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina. But, after an intervention from US President Donald Trump, the one-match ban was suspended for 12 months.

news.title: Will FIFA replay the World Cup final? Argentina petition tops 80,000 signatures
news.lede: A growing online movement is calling on FIFA to replay the 2026 FIFA World Cup final after Spain's 1-0 victory over Argentina, but experts say the chances of that happening remain extremely slim. An online Change.org petition launched by Argentine supporter Gisela Sánchez has attracted more than 80,000 signatures, according to multiple reports, after fans questioned several refereeing decisions made during the championship match at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey on July 19. The petition asks FIFA to review the match and consider a replay, alleging that officiating decisions influenced the outcome. Spain captured the World Cup title thanks to an extra-time goal from Ferran Torres, defeating defending champion Argentina 1-0 in one of the tournament's most dramatic matches.
